Footer.tagline: PDF to Markdown · all conversions run locally in your browser, your files never leave your device.

Converter.dropSubtitle: 100% local processing · Files never leave your browser · Max 50MB
Converter.errorTooLarge: File exceeds 50MB. Larger files are not supported yet.
Converter.warningNoText: No text could be extracted. This may be a scanned or image-only PDF. OCR support is on the roadmap.

Home.metaTitle: PDF to Markdown — Free, Private, Local Converter
Home.metaDescription: Convert PDF to clean Markdown right in your browser. 100% local — files never leave your device. Built for LLM, RAG, Obsidian and Notion workflows.
Home.subtitle: Convert PDF to clean Markdown right in your browser. Built for LLM, RAG, Obsidian and Notion workflows.

features.title: Private by design
features.body: Everything happens in your browser. No uploads, no servers, no tracking of your documents.

features.title: Clean structure
features.body: Headings, lists, nested bullets and tables are preserved so the Markdown is ready to use.

features.title: Free & unlimited
features.body: No sign-up, no watermark, no daily limit for single-file conversions.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded to a server?
faqs.a: No. Conversion runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF never leaves your device, which makes it safe for contracts, research and other private documents.
Headings, paragraphs, ordered and unordered lists (including nesting) and tables. Running headers, footers and page numbers are stripped automatically.
Text-based PDFs are fully supported today. OCR for scanned or image-only PDFs is on the roadmap.

Obsidian.metaTitle: PDF to Markdown for Obsidian — Offline & Clean
Obsidian.metaDescription: Turn PDFs into clean Markdown notes for Obsidian. 100% offline in your browser — drop the .md straight into your vault with headings, lists and tables intact.
Obsidian.subtitle: Convert PDFs into clean, offline Markdown notes you can drop straight into your Obsidian vault — headings, lists and tables kept intact.

features.title: Vault-ready output
features.body: Download a .md file and move it into your vault. Headings become note structure and tables stay as Markdown tables.

features.title: Fully offline
features.body: No upload step means it works even with the network off — ideal for private research notes and clippings.

features.title: Clean notes
features.body: Page numbers and repeated headers/footers are removed so your notes are not polluted with print artifacts.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded to a server?
faqs.a: No. Conversion runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF never leaves your device, which makes it safe for contracts, research and other private documents.

faqs.q: How do I import the result into Obsidian?
faqs.a: Click “Download .md” and move the file into any folder inside your Obsidian vault. It appears instantly as a new note.

faqs.q: Will tables and lists survive the import?
faqs.a: Yes. Tables are converted to GitHub-flavored Markdown tables and nested bullet lists keep their indentation.

ChatGPT.metaTitle: PDF to Markdown for ChatGPT, LLMs & RAG
ChatGPT.metaDescription: Convert PDFs to clean Markdown that’s ready for ChatGPT, Claude and RAG pipelines. Compact, structured output that LLMs parse reliably — processed locally in your browser.
ChatGPT.h1: PDF to Markdown for ChatGPT & LLMs
ChatGPT.subtitle: Convert PDFs into compact, structured Markdown that ChatGPT, Claude and RAG pipelines can parse reliably — all processed locally in your browser.

features.title: Token-efficient
features.body: Markdown is far more compact than raw PDF text dumps, so you fit more context into the model’s window.

features.title: Structure the model understands
features.body: Clean headings and lists give the model document hierarchy, which improves grounding and reduces hallucination.

features.title: Ready for RAG
features.body: Predictable Markdown is easy to chunk and embed into a vector database for retrieval augmented generation.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded to a server?
faqs.a: No. Conversion runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF never leaves your device, which makes it safe for contracts, research and other private documents.

faqs.q: Why is Markdown better than raw PDF text for LLMs?
faqs.a: Markdown encodes structure (headings, lists, tables) with very few extra tokens, which helps the model interpret the document and keeps prompts small.

faqs.q: Can I use the output in a RAG pipeline?
faqs.a: Yes. The clean Markdown is straightforward to split into chunks and embed for retrieval, with headings acting as natural chunk boundaries.

Table.metaTitle: PDF Table to Markdown — Convert Tables Cleanly
Table.metaDescription: Extract tables from PDFs into GitHub-flavored Markdown tables. Column structure is detected automatically and processed locally in your browser — no upload.
keywords: extract table from pdf to markdown
Table.subtitle: Extract tables from your PDFs into clean GitHub-flavored Markdown tables. Column boundaries are detected automatically, all in your browser.

features.title: Automatic column detection
features.body: The engine aligns cells by their horizontal position, so multi-column tables come out correctly structured.

features.title: GFM tables
features.body: Output uses standard GitHub-flavored Markdown tables that render in GitHub, Obsidian, Notion and most editors.

features.title: Mixed documents welcome
features.body: Tables, headings and prose in the same PDF are all handled together in a single pass.

faqs.q: Are my files uploaded to a server?
faqs.a: No. Conversion runs entirely in your browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF never leaves your device, which makes it safe for contracts, research and other private documents.
Text-based tables with clear column spacing convert best. Merged cells are flattened because Markdown tables don’t support cell spanning.

faqs.q: Can I copy just the table?
faqs.a: Yes. Convert the PDF, switch to the source view, and copy the table block you need.

PrivacyPolicy.metaDescription: Learn how PDF to Markdown handles your data. PDF conversion runs locally in your browser; we use Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity for site usage insights.
PrivacyPolicy.intro: PDF to Markdown (pdftomarkdown.run) is a free browser-based tool that converts PDF files to Markdown. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and the choices available to you.

sections.title: 1. PDF conversion — your files stay on your device
paragraphs: When you convert a PDF, all processing happens entirely in your web browser using WebAssembly. Your PDF files are never uploaded to our servers or any third-party service for conversion.
We do not store, access, or retain the content of your PDF files or the Markdown output you generate. Once you close the browser tab, that data is gone from our perspective.

sections.title: 2. Information we collect automatically
paragraphs: When you visit our website, we and our analytics partners may automatically collect certain technical and usage information, such as:
bullets: Pages visited, links clicked, and time spent on the site
Browser type, operating system, device type, and screen resolution
Approximate geographic location (country/region level, derived from IP address)
Session interactions such as scrolls, clicks, and mouse movements (via Microsoft Clarity)

sections.title: 3. Analytics services we use
paragraphs: We use the following third-party analytics tools to understand how visitors use our site and to improve the product. These services may set cookies or similar technologies in your browser.
bullets: Google Analytics (GA4) — provided by Google LLC. Google Analytics collects data about how you interact with our website, including pages viewed and events triggered. Data is processed according to Google's privacy policy. You can learn more at https://policies.google.com/privacy and opt out via the Google Analytics Opt-out Browser Add-on at https://tools.google.com/dlpage/gaoptout.

Microsoft Clarity — provided by Microsoft Corporation. Clarity uses cookies and similar technologies to record session replays, heatmaps, and interaction data to help us understand user experience and fix usability issues. Data is processed according to Microsoft's privacy policy. You can learn more at https://privacy.microsoft.com/en-us/privacystatement.

sections.title: 4. Cookies and similar technologies
paragraphs: Our analytics providers use cookies, local storage, and similar tracking technologies to collect usage data described above. You can control cookies through your browser settings — most browsers allow you to block or delete cookies.
Disabling cookies may not affect the core PDF conversion functionality, which runs entirely in your browser without requiring cookies.

sections.title: 5. How we use collected information
paragraphs: We use the automatically collected information to:
bullets: Monitor site performance and fix errors
Understand which features and pages are most useful
Improve the user experience and prioritize product development
Detect and prevent abuse or fraudulent activity

sections.title: 6. Data sharing
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal information. Usage data collected by Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity is shared with those respective providers as described in their privacy policies.
We may also disclose information if required by law, court order, or to protect the rights, property, or safety of our users or the public.

sections.title: 7. Data retention
paragraphs: Analytics data is retained according to the retention policies of Google Analytics and Microsoft Clarity. PDF files and conversion output are never stored on our servers.

sections.title: 8. Your rights and choices
paragraphs: Depending on your location, you may have rights to access, correct, or delete personal data held about you. You can also:
bullets: Opt out of Google Analytics using the browser add-on linked above
Block or delete cookies via your browser settings
Use a privacy-focused browser or extensions to limit tracking

sections.title: 9. Children's privacy
paragraphs: Our service is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

sections.title: 10. Changes to this policy
paragraphs: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we do, we will revise the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

sections.title: 11. Contact
paragraphs: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at privacy@pdftomarkdown.run.
